name: Immune-Mediated Polyarthritis (IMPA)
description: IMPA is a non-erosive inflammatory joint disease caused by immune complex deposition in synovial membranes. Dogs present with multiple swollen, painful joints and systemic illness. It is usually idiopathic but can be secondary to infection or neoplasia.
treatment_overview: Immunosuppressive prednisolone is highly effective for idiopathic type I IMPA. Secondary forms require treatment of the underlying cause. Long-term tapering doses prevent relapse.
prevention_tips: Treat infectious diseases promptly to prevent immune-complex deposition
